patterns_discovered.description: Translation files use hierarchical JSON structure with nested keys for logical grouping (e.g., auth.login, auth.register, days.monday)
Strings include template variables like {{organization}}, {{year}}, {{min}}, {{max}} for dynamic content insertion
Errors categorized by type: HTTP, authentication, authorization, validation, business logic, rate limiting, server, and modules
Uses proper Turkish grammar and conventions including proper noun capitalization (E-Menum), appropriate use of suffixes (-li, -lı, -lu, -lü), and formal polite language
Common UI actions (save, cancel, delete, edit) are centralized in common.json for consistency across the application

gotchas_discovered.description: Turkish language uses special characters (ç, ğ, ı, ö, ş, ü) which must be properly encoded in JSON files
Turkish has complex pluralization rules that differ from English; translation files may need separate singular/plural forms
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential issues with dynamic content generation

gotchas_discovered.description: Turkish date/time formatting differs from English (e.g., DD.MM.YYYY vs MM/DD/YYYY)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Must be handled in date formatting logic, not just translation strings
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Foundation laid with days and months translations

gotchas_discovered.description: The git diff output was truncated at 26104 characters, making it impossible to verify menu.json and order.json contents
Template variables use {{brackets}} syntax; frontend implementation must support this interpolation pattern
gotchas_discovered.impact: Integration dependency on template engine support

approach_outcome.execution_notes: Turkish translation files were successfully created with comprehensive coverage of authentication, common UI elements, and error messages. The implementation follows a hierarchical JSON structure pattern consistent with internationalization (i18n) best practices.
approach_outcome.testing_status: Not explicitly documented in session data

recommendations.recommendation: Create a Turkish language style guide documenting proper formatting conventions, technical term translations, and tone guidelines for consistency across future translations
Implement automated tests to verify all translation keys are complete across all language files (Turkish, English, etc.) and no keys are missing
Verify the contents of menu.json and order.json translations as the git diff was truncated and these files could not be fully reviewed
Implement proper locale configuration for date/time formatting, currency display (TL), and number formatting specific to Turkish locale
Establish a translation management workflow to ensure new strings added to English translations are propagated to Turkish translations promptly
Conduct native Turkish speaker review of technical terminology translations, especially in menu and order management domains, to ensure business logic clarity
